title: Your First Episode
wikitext:
[]: Your First Episode             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Fri Jan 16, 2009 11:47 pm What was the first epiosde that you saw first in the series? For me I think it was Episode 8 back in 1996 or 1997. What about you?

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Fri Jan 16, 2009 11:49 pm Music Video, I Think I'm A Clone Now, but first full episode was 1, about 7-9 months after I saw the video.

[]:             Quote Post by DavidSeth2 » Sat Jan 17, 2009 1:21 am ep 25 =/ its funny how you found eva through that weird al amv, where as i found weird al through that very same eva amv lol.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Sat Jan 17, 2009 9:42 am Episode 1 or 2(can't remember) on AdultSwim.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Jan 17, 2009 9:44 am Ep 7 ...yeah, I kinda went like "what's the big deal with this show"

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Sat Jan 17, 2009 11:20 am I believe that the episode with Leliel was the first episode I saw, but when I saw episodes 25-26 for the first time, I had a very strong sense that I'd seen them somewhere before a long time before. I dunno :/

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Sat Jan 17, 2009 12:13 pm Episode 9. Then I made the fatal error of watching EoE right after.

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Sun Jan 18, 2009 8:03 am Death and Rebirth was the first thing. Then episode one.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Jan 18, 2009 12:14 pm First one I saw was the dub version of ep23 (on VHS!). A friend had it in when I came over, and it really blew me away when 'that blue-haired chick' blew herself up to save 'that scrawny guy'. When my pal found out I hadn't seen any of NGE before he immediately yanked that tape out (well before Ritsuko's confessions) and started me off at the beginning. It definately colored my opinions of the show, as I saw it as a tragic Shinji/Rei romance and thus rather disliked Asuka right from the start for her role as Rei's rival. It's worth noting that the only anime I'd seen at the time were of the mecha and comedy/harem genres... NGE was quite an eye-opening experience!

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jan 18, 2009 12:29 pm I managed the unusual feat of having started with episode 1, totally unspoilt, and worked through in order, following up the series with EoE. It still looked like a Shinji/Rei romance to me -- at least up to such a time as the story had gone well beyond such territory (about episode 16).

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Sun Jan 18, 2009 1:15 pm Mr. Tines wrote: I managed the unusual feat of having started with episode 1, totally unspoilt, and worked through in order, following up the series with EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Sun Jan 18, 2009 2:33 pm EoE...yeah, not a good idea. I just sort of thought 'that wus feckin weird...butAWESOME!! NEEDMOAR!!!' and bought the series after that.

[]:             Quote Post by MugwumpHasNoLiver » Mon Jan 19, 2009 3:08 am I think it had to have been two or three when it first aired on adult swim. I watched maybe five minutes, saw a chick with short-shorts and a penguin doing wacky things and immediately dismissed it as some juvenile shonen-type crap. And then maybe a month later I caught ep 24 on late at night and was just kind of listening to it as I was folding laundry. By this time I'd caught a couple minutes of some of the later episodes and was intrigued. I saw Shinji taken into Leliel and crush Kaworu. I didn't know the significance of any of it, but it made me realize that I'd probably judged the show too quickly. Then 24 sealed it for me, I loved the surreal, minimalist psychoanalytical angle. So, I decided when it was re-run I was going to watch every episode in order. Now, I can't take most other anime seriously.

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Mon Jan 19, 2009 4:08 am End of Evangelion first.... then around to 1 and straight through, except 4 and 5. It's like taking The Dark Tower and reading coda(found) then slinging right back to the start... sort of.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Mon Jan 19, 2009 6:49 am First episode I saw was Magma Diver back in '07. I thought to myself "This show looks like a bit of fun!" How wrong I was...

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Mon Jan 19, 2009 12:08 pm episode 26 back when Eva was on Adult Swim

[]:             Quote Post by Gamer_2k4 » Mon Jan 19, 2009 5:35 pm THE Hal E. Burton 9000 wrote: episode 26 back when Eva was on Adult Swim

[]:             Quote Post by Annihilationscape » Mon Jan 19, 2009 7:25 pm The first episode WAS my first episode. I found the Platinum editions of the series at a video store for like 50 bucks, and I was like, "I'm in the mood for something new..." and bought it on a whim. I did not regret it.

[]:             Quote Post by Hellfire » Mon Jan 19, 2009 7:42 pm Xard wrote: Ep 7 ...yeah, I kinda went like "what's the big deal with this show"

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Mon Jan 19, 2009 8:10 pm Mr. Tines wrote: I managed the unusual feat of having started with episode 1, totally unspoilt, and worked through in order, following up the series with EoE.
